method: LLM-based semantic clustering over framing_pattern + key_finding + gap_statement across 46 facets; no external embedding API. Each facet assigned to the single best-fitting cluster based on a joint judgment of (domain, methodology, ideation_type, study population). Borderline cases resolved by the dominant framing rather than topical keyword overlap.

clusters.label: PCIA-항암유발 외모변화 (RCT/cohort/도구)
clusters.description: Persistent chemotherapy-induced alopecia and broader chemotherapy-induced appearance change (hair, skin, glabellar lines) in breast cancer patients. Uses an interlocking program of Folliscope-based objective measurement, prospective cohorts, single-arm and randomized trials of preventive/therapeutic interventions (scalp cooling, topical lotions, moisturizers), and condition-specific PROMs of altered-appearance distress.
clusters.exemplar_reason: Scalp Cooling RCT (JAMA Oncol 2024) is the most representative node: it converts the group's PCIA observational program (30120165, 41490113) into a randomized preventive trial using objective Folliscope endpoints and CADS distress, the same instruments and population that anchor the entire cluster.

clusters.label: 유방암 생존자 코호트·심리사회·삶의 질
clusters.description: Breast cancer survivorship outcomes in single- or multi-center prospective/cross-sectional cohorts: body image after treatment, divorce, return-to-work, working-status change, financial toxicity, happiness/spirituality, mind-body education, and ML-based long-term QoL prediction. Anchored in BIG-S and the post-treatment Korean survivor population rather than nationwide claims.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The BIG-S cohort profile paper is the backbone for this cluster's data infrastructure: integrating PROs, EMR, body composition, and healthcare utilization in an Asian survivor cohort, which is the methodological frame that the cross-sectional/RCT survivorship papers around it operationalize.

clusters.label: NHIS 만성질환·이차암 종단 코호트
clusters.description: Population-scale K-NHIS nationwide claims studies linking breast cancer (or maternal cardiovascular disease) to long-term incidence of chronic disease and second malignancy, using matched comparator cohorts, screening-linked baseline risk factors, and time-varying exposures. Hard-outcome survivorship epidemiology rather than PRO-driven survivorship.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The temporal-patterns-of-chronic-disease-after-breast-cancer paper most cleanly exemplifies the cluster: 84,969 K-NHIS breast cancer cases vs 1M+ matched controls with screening-linked baseline risks and time-varying exposures, the exact template reused for NHL risk (34907177) and extended to maternal ASCVD (41383953).

clusters.label: NHIS 심혈관·약물 target trial emulation
clusters.description: Korean NHIS-based real-world cardiovascular and psychopharmacology causal-inference studies (post-PCI LDL targets, DAPT + PPI, autologous vs implant breast reconstruction and mental disorders, low-dose olanzapine real-world dosing). Framed as resolving guideline inconsistencies or RCT-evidence gaps via target trial emulation or PS-matched nationwide cohorts.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The AR-vs-IBR target trial emulation paper is the clearest archetype: explicit target trial emulation on K-NHIS to settle inconsistent prior evidence, identical framing pattern to the LDL post-PCI (40294950) and PPI+DAPT (39962947) NHIS papers.

clusters.label: 흡연·전자담배 contrarian editorials/cohort
clusters.description: European Heart Journal editorials and one nationwide cohort on combustible cigarettes, e-cigarette switching after PCI, and the 'less harmful alternative' debate. Editorials explicitly contest harm-reduction framing; the empirical paper provides post-PCI MACE evidence on switching trajectories.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The 'addressing concerns on the observational study of electronic cigarettes' editorial captures the cluster's contrarian framing most directly: it methodologically defends e-cigarette observational evidence and contests the 'less harmful alternative' narrative, the same tension underlying the smoking editorial (39775792) and the post-PCI switching cohort (39429032).

clusters.label: Young Breast Cancer (CHARM/SMC YBC) 시리즈
clusters.description: Young (<=40) breast cancer cohort series built around the CHARM/SMC YBC registry: cohort profile and serial analyses of pretreatment endocrine symptoms and social-support change as predictors of recurrence-free survival. Repeated use of FACT-G/FACT-ES at diagnosis and during treatment with Cox modeling.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The CHARM cohort profile paper defines the data platform (n=1,868, multi-omics, mobile PROs, 15-year follow-up) on which both downstream RFS papers (37547446, 37669709) directly draw, making it the most representative anchor.

clusters.label: PRO 도구 자가제작/검증 (COSMIN 파이프라인)
clusters.description: Self-developed and translated patient-reported outcome instruments validated through a COSMIN-style pipeline (qualitative + cognitive interviews + EFA/CFA + convergent/known-group validity): MRI-DS, CaSUN-K, K-PROMIS-29 in cancer and CPD. Same methodological template applied across diseases.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The K-PROMIS-29 V2.1 in mixed cancer outpatients is the prototypical execution of the template (Cronbach's alpha, EFA/CFA, EORTC convergent validity) that is then ported almost identically to CPD (34795924), NSCLC (35209702), and MRI-related distress (39126264).

clusters.label: PRO 기반 supportive care·증상 감시·심리사회
clusters.description: PRO-driven symptom surveillance, distress screening, supportive care effectiveness, salivary gland and HCC HRQoL, NHL survivorship, body image after HSCT, perceived stress and NAFLD. Span cross-sectional symptom mapping (PRO-CTCAE), HRQoL trajectory analysis, target trial emulation of distress screening, and a systematic review. Unified by use of routinely collected PROs to characterize symptom and psychosocial burden across heterogeneous diseases beyond the breast-cancer survivor core.
clusters.exemplar_reason: The PRO-CTCAE real-world symptom surveillance paper (1,352 outpatients, 10 cancer-specific subsets, linked to EORTC QLQ-C30 HRQoL) most directly typifies the cluster's logic of using PROs to profile symptom burden across diverse cancer types and translate it into supportive-care implications.

clusters.label: Erratum / 기타
clusters.description: Non-substantive author correction notice without new methods or data; retained for completeness.
clusters.exemplar_reason: Only member of the cluster; the erratum corrects swapped figure legends in the NAFLD-perceived stress paper (31913326), so it is meta-content of C8 rather than substantive research.

notes: All 46 facets assigned to exactly one cluster (sum of n = 46, unclustered = 0). Edge cases: (1) 38484885 (distress-screening target trial emulation) is methodologically aligned with C4 (target trial emulation) but its supportive-care/PRO substantive focus and Seoul cancer-center data source place it in C8. (2) 40294950 and 39962947 sit at the C3/C4 boundary; both use K-NHIS but are foregrounded as guideline-resolution causal-inference studies, hence C4. (3) 34507971 (breast density trajectory) and 35174421 (financial toxicity) sit at the C2/C3 boundary; both use single/multi-center cohorts rather than NHIS, so C2. (4) 41383953 (maternal ASCVD K-NHIS mother-child paired cohort) is C3 because its framing is population-scale linked claims, not target trial emulation. (5) C5 is small (n=3) but coherent and intentionally separated from C4 because of its contrarian editorial voice and harm-reduction policy framing distinct from causal-inference cardiovascular work.
